gene_region.label: MFN2 transcribed interval

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: MFN2 encodes a mitochondrial outer-membrane fusion GTPase connecting axonal maintenance, ER contacts, and energy distribution. This topic bundle emphasizes obscure high-impact biology, with interpretation centered on rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local MFN2 bundle is oriented toward obscure high-impact biology. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: MFN2 variants can alter, tag, or contextualize rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.
condition_research_overview: Obscure or underappreciated genes with surprising phenotype impact
Rare disease, trait modifier, sensory, metabolic, immune, digestive, and neurobehavioral pathway review
Candidate-gene triage where exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, and phenotype can flip interpretation
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: MFN2 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for MFN2.
Because MFN2 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use MFN2 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
